AC.L2-3.1.1.guidance: Enforce unique account provisioning and device enrollment before any system access is granted.

AC.L2-3.1.2.guidance: Map user roles to permitted transaction types; enforce via RBAC.

AC.L2-3.1.3.guidance: Implement approval workflows and data-flow policies restricting CUI movement to sanctioned paths.

AC.L2-3.1.4.guidance: Split admin and end-user roles so no single person can both execute and approve a sensitive action.

AC.L2-3.1.5.guidance: Grant only the minimum access needed; use non-admin daily-driver accounts with escalation.

AC.L2-3.1.6.guidance: Require standard user accounts for routine work; reserve admin accounts for admin tasks only.

AC.L2-3.1.7.guidance: Block privilege escalation for standard users and log any escalation attempt.

AC.L2-3.1.8.guidance: Configure account lockout after a defined threshold of failed logons.

AC.L2-3.1.9.guidance: Display a DoD-approved logon banner/warning notice before authentication.

AC.L2-3.1.10.guidance: Enforce automatic screen lock after a short idle period.

AC.L2-3.1.11.guidance: Configure session timeout thresholds for cloud consoles and remote sessions.

AC.L2-3.1.12.guidance: Log and actively monitor all remote administrative sessions.

AC.L2-3.1.13.guidance: Require TLS 1.2+/VPN encryption for all remote sessions.

AC.L2-3.1.14.guidance: Force remote connections through a single managed gateway rather than direct exposure.

AC.L2-3.1.15.guidance: Require explicit approval before privileged remote actions are executed.

AC.L2-3.1.16.guidance: Maintain an explicit allow-list/authorization step before wireless devices join the network.

AC.L2-3.1.17.guidance: Use strong authentication (WPA3) and encryption for all wireless traffic.

AC.L2-3.1.18.guidance: Only allow enrolled, managed mobile devices to connect to CUI-adjacent systems.

AC.L2-3.1.19.guidance: Ensure full-disk/app-level encryption on all mobile endpoints handling CUI.

AC.L2-3.1.20.guidance: Document and restrict every external system connection point (e.g., only PreVeil↔AWS GovCloud).

AC.L2-3.1.21.guidance: Block or tightly control USB/removable media usage, especially off managed endpoints.

AC.L2-3.1.22.guidance: Review all public-facing content for inadvertent CUI exposure on a recurring basis.

AT.L2-3.2.1.guidance: Deliver baseline security-awareness training to all personnel before system access.

AT.L2-3.2.2.guidance: Provide role-specific security training (e.g., executive vs. engineer tracks).

AT.L2-3.2.3.guidance: Deliver annual insider-threat-recognition training to all personnel.

AU.L2-3.3.1.guidance: Enable centralized logging across all in-scope systems with adequate retention.

AU.L2-3.3.2.guidance: Eliminate shared accounts; assign unique identities to every user.

AU.L2-3.3.3.guidance: Periodically review which events are logged and adjust as risk changes.

AU.L2-3.3.4.guidance: Configure automated alerting when logging services stop or fail.

AU.L2-3.3.5.guidance: Correlate logs across systems to detect coordinated or multi-stage activity.

AU.L2-3.3.6.guidance: Support exporting/summarizing logs on demand for investigations.

AU.L2-3.3.7.guidance: Sync all systems to an authoritative time source (e.g., NIST NTP).

AU.L2-3.3.8.guidance: Restrict ACLs on log files/tools to prevent tampering.

AU.L2-3.3.9.guidance: Restrict who can configure or disable logging to a small trusted group.

CM.L2-3.4.1.guidance: Maintain a documented baseline config and asset inventory.

CM.L2-3.4.2.guidance: Enforce hardened configuration baselines via policy.

CM.L2-3.4.3.guidance: Log all configuration changes and require approval for significant ones.

CM.L2-3.4.4.guidance: Require a security impact review before deploying changes.

CM.L2-3.4.5.guidance: Restrict who may modify baseline configurations.

CM.L2-3.4.6.guidance: Disable unused services, protocols, and features.

CM.L2-3.4.7.guidance: Apply default-deny firewall rules and disable unneeded ports/services.

CM.L2-3.4.9.guidance: Prevent standard users from installing unapproved software.

IA.L2-3.5.1.guidance: Assign unique identities to every user, process, and device.

IA.L2-3.5.2.guidance: Require authentication before any system access is granted.

IA.L2-3.5.3.guidance: Deploy MFA broadly — full 5-point risk if absent entirely, partial 3-point risk if only partially rolled out.

IA.L2-3.5.4.guidance: Use authentication protocols resistant to replay attacks (e.g., FIDO2/WebAuthn).

IA.L2-3.5.5.guidance: Do not recycle usernames/IDs for a defined retention window.

IA.L2-3.5.6.guidance: Automatically disable dormant accounts after a set period.

IA.L2-3.5.8.guidance: Enforce password history to block reuse.

IA.L2-3.5.9.guidance: Force password change on first login for temporary credentials.
tools: Windows "must change at next logon" flag

IA.L2-3.5.10.guidance: Ensure passwords are hashed/encrypted in storage and transit.

IR.L2-3.6.1.guidance: Maintain a documented incident-response plan with defined roles.

IR.L2-3.6.2.guidance: Report qualifying cyber incidents to DoD within 72 hours (DFARS 252.204-7012).

IR.L2-3.6.3.guidance: Run periodic tabletop exercises testing the IR plan.

MA.L2-3.7.1.guidance: Conduct routine patching/maintenance on a defined cadence.

MA.L2-3.7.2.guidance: Restrict maintenance tooling to vetted, approved tools.

MA.L2-3.7.3.guidance: Wipe CUI from any device before it leaves the CUI boundary for repair.

MA.L2-3.7.4.guidance: Scan maintenance media for malware before use.

MA.L2-3.7.5.guidance: Require MFA for any remote maintenance session, terminating promptly after use.

MA.L2-3.7.6.guidance: Supervise unescorted/unauthorized maintenance personnel while they work on in-scope systems.

MP.L2-3.8.1.guidance: Physically secure and control all media (paper/digital) containing CUI.

MP.L2-3.8.2.guidance: Restrict access to CUI-bearing media to named authorized users.
tools: Access control lists on shared storage

MP.L2-3.8.3.guidance: Apply NIST 800-88 clear/purge/destroy procedures before disposal or reuse.

MP.L2-3.8.4.guidance: Apply required CUI banner/portion markings to all media.

MP.L2-3.8.5.guidance: Track custody and control of CUI media whenever it leaves controlled areas.

MP.L2-3.8.6.guidance: Encrypt CUI on any digital media transported outside controlled areas.

MP.L2-3.8.7.guidance: Block or tightly restrict removable media (USB) use.

MP.L2-3.8.9.guidance: Encrypt and secure CUI backups at rest.

PS.L2-3.9.1.guidance: Conduct background screening before granting CUI access.

PS.L2-3.9.2.guidance: Revoke CUI access promptly on termination/role change.

PE.L2-3.10.1.guidance: Restrict physical facility access to authorized personnel only.

PE.L2-3.10.2.guidance: Monitor the facility and its supporting infrastructure.

PE.L2-3.10.3.guidance: Escort all visitors and actively monitor their activity near CUI systems.

PE.L2-3.10.4.guidance: Log all physical access events to CUI-adjacent spaces.

PE.L2-3.10.5.guidance: Manage and control keys, badges, and access devices.

PE.L2-3.10.6.guidance: Apply the same physical safeguards at alternate/remote work sites.

RA.L2-3.11.1.guidance: Conduct a periodic (at least annual) formal risk assessment.

RA.L2-3.11.2.guidance: Run recurring vulnerability scans, and ad hoc scans on new CVE disclosures.

RA.L2-3.11.3.guidance: Remediate identified vulnerabilities within risk-based SLA timeframes.

CA.L2-3.12.1.guidance: Perform recurring internal assessments of control effectiveness.

CA.L2-3.12.2.guidance: Maintain a living operational plan of action for known deficiencies.
tools: POA&M / operational plan of action document

CA.L2-3.12.3.guidance: Continuously monitor controls, not just at assessment time.

CA.L2-3.12.4.guidance: Maintain a current, versioned System Security Plan (SSP).

SC.L2-3.13.1.guidance: Deploy boundary firewalls/gateways monitoring all external traffic.

SC.L2-3.13.2.guidance: Design systems with security-by-design architecture principles.

SC.L2-3.13.3.guidance: Keep admin/management functions logically separate from user-facing functions.

SC.L2-3.13.4.guidance: Ensure OS-level isolation prevents data leakage across shared resources.

SC.L2-3.13.5.guidance: Isolate public-facing systems (e.g., website) from the internal/CUI network.

SC.L2-3.13.6.guidance: Default-deny firewall posture, allowing only explicitly approved traffic.

SC.L2-3.13.7.guidance: Disable split tunneling; require full-tunnel VPN.

SC.L2-3.13.9.guidance: Auto-terminate idle network sessions after a defined timeout.

SC.L2-3.13.10.guidance: Manage cryptographic key lifecycle (generation, rotation, retirement).

SC.L2-3.13.11.guidance: Use FIPS 140-2/140-3 validated cryptographic modules for all CUI encryption.

SC.L2-3.13.12.guidance: Disable remote camera/mic activation; provide in-use indicators.

SC.L2-3.13.13.guidance: Restrict/monitor browser scripting and other mobile code execution.
tools: Browser script-disable policy for CUI portals

SC.L2-3.13.14.guidance: Restrict CUI-related voice communications to approved, secured channels.

SC.L2-3.13.15.guidance: Ensure sessions cannot be spoofed/impersonated (mutual TLS, cert validation).

SC.L2-3.13.16.guidance: Encrypt CUI at rest on all storage systems.

SI.L2-3.14.1.guidance: Patch systems promptly upon flaw discovery.

SI.L2-3.14.2.guidance: Deploy anti-malware at endpoints and network boundaries.

SI.L2-3.14.3.guidance: Subscribe to and act on relevant security advisories (CISA KEV, US-CERT).

SI.L2-3.14.5.guidance: Run scheduled full scans plus real-time scan-on-access.
tools: Weekly full scan + real-time AV scanning

SI.L2-3.14.6.guidance: Deploy a SIEM/monitoring capability watching inbound/outbound traffic for attack indicators.

SI.L2-3.14.7.guidance: Detect and flag unauthorized system usage through log review.
